title: Coarse graining from within: Wilson-Fisher universality on $S^3$
authors: Alfio M. Bonanno, Renata Ferrero, Giovanni Oglialoro
abstract: Wilsonian renormalization is usually formulated in momentum space, but on curved backgrounds momentum shells have no invariant meaning. We replace them by an intrinsic spectral cutoff, ordering modes by the covariant Laplacian and setting the cutoff resolution by the system size in renormalization group (RG) units. For a scalar field on $S^3$, this yields a covariant, momentum-free RG flow whose trace is an exact sum over spherical harmonics. The standard flat-space flow is recovered when the sphere is large compared with the coarse-graining scale. As a nontrivial test, the compact spectral flow realizes Wilson-Fisher universality without momentum shells: the interacting fixed point survives at finite resolution, has one relevant direction, and approaches its flat-space counterpart smoothly, with critical exponents only weakly affected by the compact spectrum.
summary: This work develops a method for performing Wilsonian renormalization group flow on curved spaces, specifically using the three-sphere ($S^3$). Instead of relying on momentum shells, the authors use an intrinsic spectral cutoff based on the covariant Laplacian spectrum. They successfully show that the critical physics, characterized by the Wilson-Fisher fixed point, remains robust and smoothly connects to the standard flat-space results.
main_problem: The paper addresses the challenge of formulating Wilsonian renormalization group (RG) flow on curved backgrounds where the concept of momentum shells is ill-defined.
main_result: The Wilson-Fisher fixed point is shown to persist on the compact three-sphere ($S^3$) using an intrinsic spectral cutoff, demonstrating that RG flow can be defined purely by the spectral structure of the covariant Laplacian.
method: The authors employ a proper-time functional RG flow, replacing momentum-space cutoffs with an intrinsic spectral cutoff ordered by the covariant Laplacian eigenvalues.
model_or_system: The system analyzed is a single real scalar field theory defined on the three-sphere ($S^3$), a compact curved manifold.
key_observables:
Critical exponents ($
u, \omega$), the Wilson-Fisher fixed point, and the dependence of these quantities on the spectral cutoff resolution parameter ($	au$).
important_parameters: The spectral cutoff resolution parameter ($	au = ka$), the system size (related to the sphere's curvature), and the regulator choice parameter ($m$).
main_assumptions: The analysis utilizes the Local Potential Approximation (LPA) for the RG flow, and the core assumption is that the spectral structure itself dictates the physical ordering of fluctuations.
figures_summary:
Figure 1 illustrates that critical exponents ($
u$ and $\omega$) approach their known flat-space values as the intrinsic resolution parameter ($	au$) increases, confirming the smooth connection to the continuum limit.
paper_structure: The paper first establishes the necessity of an intrinsic spectral cutoff for curved spaces. It then develops the RG flow equation using the covariant Laplacian spectrum on $S^3$. Finally, it analyzes the fixed point structure, confirming its stability and smooth approach to the flat-space limit.
why_interesting: While focused on QFT, the concept of defining physical scales and universality using intrinsic spectral properties rather than momentum space is highly relevant to understanding effective theories in curved or constrained quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Intrinsic-resolution dependence of the critical expo- nents in the N = 2 truncation. Both ν and ω approach their flat-space values as τ increases. The residual finite-resolution dependence decreases as the proper-time threshold is made more localized by increasing m.
